items.a: How context contributes to meaning in communication
options: Conventional spelling system of a language
How context contributes to meaning in communication
Two letters that represent one sound
items.explanation: pragmatics means How context contributes to meaning in communication.
Use the linguistic definition of semantics.

items.q: Which linguistic term matches this definition: Using a part to represent the whole or vice versa? (set 34)

items.a: Replacing something with a related term
items.explanation: Use the linguistic definition of metonymy.

items.q: Which linguistic term matches this definition: Change in formality based on audience or purpose? (set 32)
items.explanation: The matching term is register shift.

items.a: Repetition of a word or phrase at the start of clauses
items.explanation: Use the linguistic definition of anaphora.

items.q: A bound morpheme can stand alone as a sentence.
items.explanation: It must attach to another morpheme.

items.q: Which linguistic term matches this definition: Conventional spelling system of a language? (set 30)

items.a: Words whose meaning depends on context like here or now
items.explanation: Use the linguistic definition of deixis.

items.q: Pragmatics includes implied meaning and social context.
